title: podman build --rm: differs from buildah
body:
Another slight difference between `buildah bud` and `podman build`, this time in the `--rm=false` code:
```console
$ tree foo-3
foo-3
├── Containerfile
└── testfile

0 directories, 2 files
$ cat foo-3/Containerfile
FROM quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest
ADD testfile /tmp/
ENV foo=bar
$ ./bin/podman build --layers --rm=false -t foo foo-3
STEP 1: FROM quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest
STEP 2: ADD testfile /tmp/
--> 59ead2dcc95
STEP 3: ENV foo=bar
STEP 4: COMMIT foo
--> e6b4ff10c02
e6b4ff10c022ce32182ab82940c60f21935205ff76ef9b2a83845b21c75b7809
$ buildah containers
CONTAINER ID  BUILDER  IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME                       CONTAINER NAME
687f15b13a49     *     961769676411 quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest     alpine-working-container
[ *** the 59ead layer is not listed ***]
$ ./bin/podman images -a
REPOSITORY                 TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED             SIZE
localhost/foo              latest  e6b4ff10c022  About a minute ago  5.85 MB
<none>                     <none>  59ead2dcc955  About a minute ago  5.85 MB   <--- but it is here
quay.io/libpod/alpine      latest  961769676411  18 months ago       5.85 MB
```
Compare to buildah:
```console
$ buildah rm -a        ! clean up
$ podman rmi foo       ! cleanup
$ buildah bud --layers --rm=false -t foo foo-3
STEP 1: FROM quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest
STEP 2: ADD testfile /tmp/
--> 3768eff35c7
STEP 3: ENV foo=bar
STEP 4: COMMIT foo
--> ff4a6a5dbaa
ff4a6a5dbaa9a71c3875e15ac617b92a8ac9baf2adf58f680c6e4daa8346de19
$ buildah containers
CONTAINER ID  BUILDER  IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME                       CONTAINER NAME
e2b72d0b23ac     *     961769676411 quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest     alpine-working-container
9c8259946f3f     *     3768eff35c7c                                  3768eff35c7cce8e63ad436370990a7f2c9fd5145a484150c5d793c9d27572d2-working-container
                       ^^^^^^^^^^^^ this is the 'ADD' layer
```
I don't know if this is a meaningful difference. It is causing the `bud with --rm flag` test to fail when running `bud.bats` under podman in #9296. I can easily just `skip` that test.

comments.body: This is strange.  No obvious difference between Buildah bud and Podman Build.

--force-rm                                     Always remove intermediate containers after a build, even if the build is unsuccessful. (default true)

Is different between buildah and podman.
Ah - thanks. I'm having trouble understanding how that should interact with `--rm=false`, but it's been a long day, and I'm going to punt on the question until tomorrow!

Confirmed: adding `--force-rm=false` to #9296 makes buildah tests [pass](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5195868094595072/html/bud-podman-fedora-33-root-host.log.html).
